name: Pocketr
description: Self-hosted personal finance and household budgeting application for tracking accounts, categories, ledger transactions, reports, and shared household workflows.

notes.text: Access the app at `/frontend`; the API is served from `/api/v1` on the same port.
Create the first user by registering from the web interface.
The script installs the published Pocketr release jar, which includes the built Vue frontend.

By default the script installs PostgreSQL locally. To use an existing PostgreSQL server, run with `POCKETR_DB_MODE=external`, `POCKETR_DB_URL`, `POCKETR_DB_USERNAME`, and `POCKETR_DB_PASSWORD`; the external database must already exist and be reachable.

If an external database cannot be reached during install, the LXC is kept and `pocketr.service` is enabled but not started. Edit `/opt/pocketr/.env`, then run `systemctl start pocketr`; enabled services also start on LXC reboot.
For local PostgreSQL installs, generated database credentials are saved to `~/pocketr.creds`. Runtime configuration is in `/opt/pocketr/.env`.
